01_initial_request.input_data: Four distinct application scenes and DNA base arrangements
01_initial_request.user_intent: To streamline DNA sequence design for multi-scene fusion and enhance capabilities for tailoring sequences to multiple specific applications
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Sequences meeting expected performance levels in four distinct scenes and outperforming eight swarm evolutionary algorithms on 23 benchmark functions

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] DNA sequence design requires multiple constraint models to ensure reliability and stability. Previous work used six universal models. [Gap] Multi-scene fusion requires additional constraints for ion-bridge mismatch and Hoogsteen sequences. [Decision] To achieve comprehensive DNA sequence design, chose to use constraint modeling to perform establishment of 8 constraint models on DNA sequences, expecting to obtain a complete constraint framework.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Eight constraint models established including two novel models (Ion-bridge RS and HG-quadruplex) for multi-scene fusion DNA sequence design

references.citation: Yang GJ, Wang B, Zheng XD, Zhou CJ, Zhang Q (2017)
references.relevance: Provides foundation for six universal constraint models in DNA sequence design

references.citation: Guo YJ, Yao DB, Zheng B, et al. (2020)
references.relevance: Supports Hoogsteen sequence design and pH-dependent applications

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Traditional optimization algorithms may encounter premature convergence and low global search ability. [Gap] Need an algorithm that can handle multi-scene DNA sequence optimization effectively. [Decision] To achieve rapid optimization of DNA sequences, chose to use Dynamic Virus Spread (DVS) algorithm to perform configuration with specific parameters on DNA sequence population, expecting to obtain optimized algorithm settings.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: DVS algorithm configured with 27 parameters governing iteration mode, spread scale, and DNA sequence optimization specifics

references.relevance: Provides foundation for virus spread simulation in optimization algorithms
Related virus-based optimization approach for comparison

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] DVS algorithm requires evaluation to verify its optimization capabilities. [Gap] Need to benchmark against existing swarm evolutionary algorithms. [Decision] To achieve algorithm performance validation, chose to use 23 benchmark functions to perform independent testing on DVS algorithm, expecting to obtain performance metrics across unimodal and multimodal functions.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: DVS demonstrated excellent performance in 8 functions (F9-F11, F16-F20), all concentrated in multimodal functions, and good results in unimodal functions F1-F4

references.relevance: Standard test suite for evaluating optimization algorithm performance

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Algorithm performance needs comparison with state-of-the-art methods. [Gap] Need to demonstrate DVS superiority over existing approaches. [Decision] To achieve competitive validation, chose to use 8 swarm evolutionary algorithms to perform comparative analysis against DVS on benchmark functions, expecting to obtain dominance metrics.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: DVS outperformed 8 popular swarm evolutionary algorithms with 8 dominant results and 4 suboptimal results across 23 benchmark functions

references.citation: CHIO [33], SCSO [40], MRFO [41], ASO [42], GWO [43], SSA [44], WOA [45], SCA [46]
references.relevance: Eight trendy swarm evolutionary algorithms used for performance comparison

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Multi-scene fusion requires testing across different DNA computing applications. [Gap] Need to validate sequence design for tri-base scenarios. [Decision] To achieve Scene 1 validation, chose to use DVS algorithm to perform tri-base sequence design optimization on DNA sequences, expecting to obtain low complementarity and low Tm difference sequences.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Tri-base design strategy achieved optimization objectives with low complementarity and low Tm difference, compared favorably against HSWOA, NCIWO, and IBPSO

references.relevance: Comparison methods for tri-base sequence design validation

references.citation: Li X, Wang B, Lv H, Yin Q, Zhang Q, Wei XP (2020)
references.relevance: Triplet-bases unpaired constraint for DNA sequences

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Ion-bridge mismatch sequences enable selective ion regulation pairing. [Gap] Need to validate sequence design for heavy metal ion detection. [Decision] To achieve Scene 2 validation, chose to use DVS algorithm to perform ion-bridge mismatch sequence design on DNA sequences, expecting to obtain controllable complementary pairing sequences.

parameters.application: Selective ion regulation pairing, heavy metal ion detection
parameters.hairpin_specification: Specify position and quantity of hairpins

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Ion-bridge mismatched hairpin sequences designed successfully, enabling flexible creation of ion-bridge hairpins at biologically aligned sites for heavy metal ion detection

references.citation: Wei Y, Li BM, Wang X, Duan YX (2014)
references.relevance: Silver ion detection using DNA hybrid systems

references.citation: Reference sequences I1 [48], I2 [25], I3 [49], I4 [50]
references.relevance: Previous ion-bridge sequences for validation comparison

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Restriction endonuclease recognition domains enable molecular logic gates and signal amplification. [Gap] Need to validate sequence design with enzyme recognition domains. [Decision] To achieve Scene 3 validation, chose to use DVS algorithm to perform restriction endonuclease recognition domain design on DNA sequences, expecting to obtain low complementarity sequences with enzyme domains.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Sequence design results with four common restriction endonuclease recognition domains achieved low complementarity characteristics, useful for multi-enzyme DNA reaction systems

references.citation: Pan LQ, Wang ZY, Li YF, et al. (2017)
references.relevance: Nicking enzyme-controlled toehold regulation for DNA logic circuits

references.citation: Zhang XK, Zhang Q, Liu Y, et al. (2020)
references.relevance: DNA molecular lock driven by nicking enzymes

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Hoogsteen sequences enable pH-dependent applications with high sensitivity. [Gap] Need to validate sequence design for pH-responsive systems. [Decision] To achieve Scene 4 validation, chose to use DVS algorithm to perform Hoogsteen sequence design on DNA sequences, expecting to obtain pH-sensitive triplet structures.

parameters.application: pH-dependent applications, highly sensitive pH responses

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Hoogsteen sequences designed successfully with minimized continuous guanine content in Pu sequence, reducing G4 structure formation likelihood while maintaining pH sensitivity

references.citation: Guo YJ, Yao DB, Zheng B, et al. (2020)
references.relevance: pH-controlled detachable DNA circuitry and Hoogsteen triplet design

references.citation: Qi HJ, Yue SZ, Bi S, et al. (2018)
references.relevance: DNA logic assembly powered by triplex-helix molecular switch for pH imaging

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Multi-scene DNA sequence design validation with benchmark function testing and algorithm comparison

benchmark_functions_tested.interpretation: Well-known benchmark functions used to evaluate DVS algorithm performance

dominant_results.interpretation: Number of benchmark functions where DVS outperformed all 8 comparison algorithms

suboptimal_results.interpretation: Number of benchmark functions where DVS achieved suboptimal but competitive results

comparison_algorithms.interpretation: Number of swarm evolutionary algorithms compared against DVS (CHIO, SCSO, MRFO, ASO, GWO, SSA, WOA, SCA)

application_scenes.interpretation: Number of distinct DNA computing application scenes validated (tri-base, ion-bridge, endonuclease, Hoogsteen)

constraint_models.interpretation: Total constraint models established including 2 novel models (Ion-bridge RS, HG-quadruplex)

total_iterations.interpretation: Total maximum iterations (T×E) for DVS algorithm optimization

03_success_verification.final_verdict: Research objective achieved: DNA sequence design model for multi-scene fusion successfully developed with DVS algorithm outperforming 8 swarm evolutionary algorithms on 23 benchmark functions (8 dominant, 4 suboptimal results) and meeting expected performance levels across 4 distinct application scenes (tri-base, ion-bridge, endonuclease, Hoogsteen)
